content: Postgres won't let you ADD VALUE to an enum and use the new value in the same transaction. Always do enum extensions in TWO migrations: first one adds the value, second one references it.
After every migration, regenerate Supabase types with: supabase gen types typescript --linked --schema public 2>/dev/null > lib/supabase/types.ts. Skipping this breaks TS on new columns/functions.

description: SECURITY DEFINER functions with returns table (...) where the returned column names match the underlying table's column names trigger 42702 'ambiguous column' errors at call time. The error message is unhelpful and points at the wrong line.
resolution: Rename the returned columns in the 'returns table (...)' clause so they don't shadow source-table columns. The create_room RPC fix in 20260419021009_fix_create_room_ambiguous.sql is the canonical example.
